---
title: "FW 536 — Day 1 morning lab (Probability) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 1 morning lab — Probability problem set

This template matches the morning problems (1–6) in [`problem_set.html`](problem_set.html).
Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and
upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

**Data:** Problem 2 uses `data/edna_carp_results.csv`. Keep the `data/` folder
alongside this `.Rmd` file so the `read.csv()` call resolves.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

---

# Problem 1 — Acoustic detection of tagged steelhead

> A tagged steelhead passes through a river reach with two independent acoustic
> receivers. Given the fish is present, each detects the tag with probability 0.85.
> (a) P(at least one detects)? (b) P(both | at least one)? (c) Why is (b) not 0.85?

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / interpretation:**


---

# Problem 2 — eDNA detection of invasive carp

> Prior P(carp present) = 0.20; assay sensitivity 0.92, specificity 0.97.
> (a) Posterior after one positive. (b) After two positives. (c) Effect of a 0.02 prior.
> (d) Load `data/edna_carp_results.csv` and compute each lake's posterior by updating
> once per replicate; which lakes end above 0.95 and which below 0.05?

**My work:**

```{r}
edna <- read.csv("data/edna_carp_results.csv")
head(edna)
# Your Bayes updates here (a Bayesian update function + a per-lake loop for part d)
```

**Answer / interpretation:**


---

# Problem 3 — Combining two survey indices

> Eight cameras run one week; a shared week-level detectability p ~ Beta(1.6, 6.4)
> (mean 0.20). (a) Under fixed p = 0.20, P(0 detect), mean, variance.
> (b) Simulate the shared-p model: draw one p per week, then 8 Bernoulli outcomes;
> estimate P(0 detect) and the variance. (c) Why does heterogeneity inflate both?

**My work:**

```{r}
set.seed(1)
# Your simulation here
```

**Answer / interpretation:**


---

# Problem 4 — Isolated callers along a ridgeline

> n = 30 cougars in a line; each calls independently with p = 0.3. An "isolated caller"
> calls while neither neighbor calls. Using the 28 interior cougars:
> (a) E[X] via indicator variables. (b) Verify by simulation (20,000 reps).
> (c) Would positive correlation between neighbors change the mean, the variance, or both?

**My work:**

```{r}
set.seed(1)
# Your indicator argument + simulation check here
```

**Answer / interpretation:**


---

# Problem 5 — Grizzly bear DNA from hair snares

> Detection given a bear is present p = 0.18; bears present at 60% of stations; no
> false positives. (a) P(at least one sample) at a random station. (b) P(present | at
> least one). (c) P(at least one across 3 visits), conditional on presence and marginally.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / interpretation:**


---

# Problem 6 — Fishers vote on a regulation

> 20 fishers each vote "yes" with probability 0.4; the motion passes with a majority.
> (a) Expected yes votes and variance. (b) P(motion passes). (c) Smallest group size n
> for which P(passage) drops below 1% (search numerically).

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image in an `images/` folder next
to this `.Rmd`. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every morning problem (1–6) from `problem_set.html`.
- [ ] Loaded `data/edna_carp_results.csv` for Problem 2 (kept the `data/` folder next to this file).
- [ ] Set a seed before each simulation (Problems 3 and 4).
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
